anti_claim: Materials chemistry closed-loop lab-safety replay exposes a source-faithful public Lab/Evolve failure-replay graph over body-free simulator rows: candidate materials, safety screens, simulated assays, active-learning decisions, cold replay refs, failure classes, restart points, source capsule hashes, and teachings. It does not export wetlab protocols, hazardous synthesis steps, reagent amounts, controlled or bioactive targets, live lab credentials, robot commands, private lab notebooks, live assay data, discovery claims, benchmark scores, or release authority.

source_capsule.authority_boundary: Capsule hashes prove deterministic public replay carryforward only; they are not chemistry safety proof, wetlab authority, benchmark evidence, publication permission, or release authorization.

source_clip.teaching_rule: When the candidate, safety screen, simulator assay, decision, and cold replay refs are valid, freeze those public refs and keep wetlab, robot, credential, and discovery authority outside the replay graph.

global_rule_candidates.rule: A public science replay can carry candidate, simulator, decision, failure, restart, and teaching refs as product substrate only when source clips are body-free and wetlab, credential, robot, private notebook, discovery, benchmark, publication, and release authority stay false.

nodes.contract: Accept body-free public candidate metadata and literature capsule refs.
Reject wetlab, controlled-target, credential, robot, notebook, and discovery authority.
Bind candidates to simulator-only experiment refs and cold replay refs.
Evaluate only public simulator proxy rows, never live assay data.
Choose the next simulator-only action class without robot execution.
Localize a blocked row to the nearest authority boundary.
Restart from the nearest body-free public row rather than replaying the whole graph.
Carry the source clip hash, restart point, and anti-claim together.

carryforward_contract.rule: Every reusable teaching must carry its body-free source clip, deterministic hash, restart point, and authority anti-claim.

secret_exclusion_scan.anti_claim: Scanner terms are regression sentinels for tests and declared import rows. A pass is not a complete secret scan, credential audit, release clearance, or proof that no private material exists.

scan_scope.intended_use: Detect declared synthetic sentinel tokens and classify explicit macro-import material rows before public body import.

blocked_source_refs.replacement_criteria: Import a source-faithful public paper-module extract only after raw operator voice anchors are removed or replaced by governed public-safe doctrine prose, with hashes and tests proving no keys, credentials, account/session state, provider payload bodies, browser/HUD live access, recipient-send state, credential-equivalent material, or raw operator voice entered Microcosm receipts.

Import a source-faithful public paper-module extract only after raw operator voice anchors are removed or replaced by governed public-safe doctrine prose, with hashes and tests proving no keys, credentials, account/session state, provider payload bodies, browser/HUD live access, recipient-send state, credential-equivalent material, or raw operator voice entered Microcosm receipts.

source_open_body_imports.reader_action: Open source_module_manifest.json plus source_modules/ inside the exported materials lab-safety bundle for copied Lab/Evolve replay specimen, replay graph, replay receipt, and laboratory standard bodies; receipts carry refs, hashes, counts, and verdicts only.
